reviews.review_text: Good camp facilities 8j
Very impressed with this park. Reception staff are helpful and polite and the cabin is very clean and modern !

Outside was dirty - all outside needs scrubbing, screen door wouldn't close or lock,  dirty and scuff marks on walls -  needs a new paint job inside.   Toilet roll holder falling of wall and ice trays in freezer empty.

Fantastic accommodation. Cabins are suoer clean, staff are superb friendly, laundry is great and tubs are clean.
Great value very reasonably priced. Quiet spot can't even hear the road.
Cannot fault and will stay here everytime I visit Darwin. Cannot fault and recommend 110% 👌

Cancelled our ten night stay in Darwin at the end of April due to fuel situation. We were charged one night fee to cancel 😞 As we are members of discovery parks, we are not happy. Giving one month notice, no other park from Port Augusta, all the way up charged us for cancelling. Obviously paying your membership means nothing!!

All washing machines in the closest laundry out of order & half the dryers.
Stayed 10 days & no service was provided to the cabin even though it clearly stated every 7 days on paperwork.
Only one BBQ working in the communal area- oven NOT working, TV not working also.
Huge noise from the airport & RAAF Base - some aircraft actually shook the cabin !!
Only one staff member was wonderful- Julie !!
Jim the maintenance guy was also helpful.
The rest of the staff ( including Management) were quite rude & very unwelcoming!!

Came to Darwin to get some work done on my 4x4, thought I'd give discovery parks ago. Seems a good enough place to stay has a gym and two pools. Also has a couple of camp kitchens. I'm staying in a studio apartment I think its called. Is clean and comfortable has all the bits and pieces for cooking etc. Perfect for what I want. Can even bring the pets. Is the off season so is quite, looks like a fair size park so would be busy in the dry season.
Was told there was no availability for the night when in the morning 20 sites were available, it seemed she couldn’t be bothered doing a booking after hours at 4:15pm

We stayed early October and occupancy seemed less than 10pct yet we were put immediately next to others -why? Our spot was awkward to back into due to untrimmed shrubbery. As with all Discovery Parks they post a 5kmh speed limit, but on one occasion we followed the groundsman on his electric cart only to later have him chip me for speeding by following him!
I was in powered campsite not a room. Great location and quiet area.

By far the worse place we stayed from dirty showers and toilets to rude staff. Also male cleaner just walks in and out of female shower block spoke to staff about this they asked what could they do he needs to clean they belittled me and made me feel stupid for feeling uncomfortable 😣. The 5km limit is ridiculous if you don't stick to it they will come out and scream at you as I witnessed a few times while in the pool trying to relax.
Good location, great facilities

Nice but not quiet. Great location, close to everything.

The pool is supposed to be closed at 7pm. Yet there a loud vermin children and parents still there afterwards and it isn't closed!

Maybe the after hours number could be answered to report that we had no water.
Great place to stay. Not far from the city or beach

Gr8 van park staff friendly
Not far fron town
Clean and close to the airport, shady sites

Property very close to Darwin airport and RAAF base,aircraft noise could be a problem|Theres not a little if shade in caravan site area.Swimming pools   play area and jumping pillow good for families.|We hired 2 bedroom place which had a separate room accessed via internal door.  No lounge and only a small table in each room.|Staff were helpful,  property situated on Stuart Highay so cabins back onto busy road
Nice park but OMG! Noise from the plane taking off 2-3 am and early 6 am. It's loud. Crazy

We stayed here for 2 weeks and had made 4 separate bookings as our road trip plan changed. On arrival, the staff were very nice and suggested we stay on the cheaper site for the entire stay to save us from moving around as we made 4 bookings. However, they didn’t mention the rate difference or offer any credit.

When we asked, we were told the price had changed between the time we booked and when we arrived – which wasn’t true, as the same rates were still showing online. After 30 minutes of going back and forth, management finally agreed to issue a credit, but not for the full amount.

In the end, the credit was never processed. Their T&Cs stated credits could only be used in Darwin Discovery Park, but we’ve stayed at Discovery Parks many times before and credits could always be used across locations. Also, they refused to give coins credit for the use of washing machine because “how do we know you will use the coin in our washing machine”
We are making this review not because of the credit amount but for the handling of the situation, felt dodgy and left a very poor impression.

Unfortunately, this was the worst customer service we’ve experienced at a Discovery Park.
Toll ist der Pool und die Busverbindung in die City. Ansonsten gute Ausstattung und gut gepflegt.

The park itself was a very picturesque place, 2 good pools, clean amenities block and strict on the 5kms speed limit. The only issues that we had was they had around 3 washing machines out of order for part of our stay.  Close enough to Darwin centre to not be a problem. Sites are a pretty generous in size. Just make sure that you clean the bat droppings off the awning before putting it away. Besides that we had a pretty good week there.
Lovely big powered site for our motorhome. Please of room between bays. Two pools.

A lovely park - everything in Darwin is 10 mins so it’s convenient, the major drawback is that it’s next to the airport!!! So planes leave overhead at 1-2am and then again around the 6am and it’s really loud!!! |Good amenities 2 lovely pools - the planes omg!!
Our 3rd visit to this caravan park and the friendliness and services have kept their high standards. Clean, well maintained amenities, swimming pool and gym make this park a must to visit.
Very clean and tidy Caravan Park with lots of amenities-  pool, laundry, clean ablutions.  Must say unfortunately we encountered a very grumpy staff member. Hopefully she was only having a bad day

Don’t stay here if you’re expecting a peaceful unbroken nights sleep or a sleep in in the morning. Planes ( that feel like they are going to fly through your front door) , taking off late at night,  around 2-3 am and in the early morning will prevent that. If you love the sounds of jet engines though,  then this is the place for you as the location is great (apart from being neighbours with the RAAF base and airport) as are the two pools and facilities. Staff are doing their best although I feel they are ready for a stouch when you walk through the office door considering the complaints they must receive. The poor things don’t seem too happy. Oh and don’t stay here on the Darwin show weekend either unless you love  carnival noises and the sounds of screaming girls until 11pm each night. The show ground is a friendly Neighbour too !! Something we weren't told about until we arrived. We were offered the option to leave but of course everything else was booked out so we had no other option but to stay. Come stay but bring your ear plugs.

Location really suited us. Management was very understanding when we requested a site change as we were staying for three weeks. Thank you.
Would have liked to see a little more attention given to the surrounding gardens etc as we were paying top dollar
Only discovered ‘new’ area the day before we left.  Much nicer! Serious money and effort has been put into the highway end of the park.
Hopefully working through the park and this will also solve their plumbing issues!
Two pools is a bonus.
Would stay again acknowledging the planes and the fact that all parks in Darwin are expensive.
Way too noisy, no chance for a good nights sleep. Amenities are all right, the ones close to the pool are kids showers, though. Not high enough.
Great CVP, plenty of room.

We stayed in a Superior cabin for five nights in early July.

The cabin was of a decent size and clean. It had everything that one would need for a comfortable stay. Our stay was pleasant.

The wi-fi was not strong enough for use in the cabin.

The TV reception was bad for the first three days of our stay, but this was fixed for the final two days.
Great park, fab location. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Nice big park with 2 lovely pools, gym and amenities.  Site sizes ok. Near the airport, motor racing facilities and showground.  Only about 10 minutes from Darwin city centre.

I loved our drive through site it was tidy and spacious and I didn't feel hemmed in. The ablutions are old but clean. The hot water taps are on the opposite side than usual so don't be fooled into thinking there's no hot water There are two pools both freezing but the one at the front doesn't attract the kids so peaceful for us oldies. It's close to everything in Darwin CBD but a little further from other attractions. It's expensive at $72 dollars and that's booking early  with a discount.
Noise from the airport is definitely heard but it didn't bother me or keep me awake  there was some distant noise from the speedway over the weekend but again it wasn't a bother to us.

GOUGING – DISAPPOINTED
Discovery Parks -Darwin
On the 4th of June I booked a 7-night stay 17th – 24th June. On 7th of June had to cancel booking due to issues with van & returning home. That gave 10 days’ notice to allow them to fill that site.
We were charged a full night’s fee as a cancellation fee. That was $77.86 plus a $4.91 credit card fee.
I can understand a cancellation/admin fee, & would be happy to pay at a reasonable cost, something around $20, but $82.77 I find extravagant when they have been given so much notice & would have very little trouble in rebooking that site.

We have Discovery/G’Day members & have stayed here on several previous occasions but will be finding somewhere better to stay in future!

Wow, what a great park to stay at! We couldn’t believe the location, condition, cleanliness, amenities, team and park were all so genuinely great! We would have stayed longer, however peak period kicked in and the prices nearly doubled. Worth booking this one!!
This caravan was great place plenty of facilities two swimming pools

Stayed Saturday night, impossible to sleep as locals have permission to play doof doof music all night long in the dry season. Office staff should notify us before we check in! Very small caravan sites.
Heerlijk zwembad, rustig . Wc etc schoon en netjes

Dog friendly and great location.
Stayed for 3 nights and loved it.
Will visit again!
Excellent place  roomy, very clean and tidy, staff  friendly and helpful
Excellent site with power and water. Thoroughly enjoy the swimming pool. Staff are helpful and friendly

Nous avons passé plusieurs jours dans ce camping, assez couteux. Les emplacements sont loin d'être exceptionnels.
Attention à vos denrées alimentaires dans le frigo, malgré qu'elles soient marquées, les nôtres ont finies à la poubelle.
De plus la pression dans les douches n'était pas très forte et l'eau chaude, avec les fortes chaleurs c'était bien dommage.
Nice room. Very nice park. Amenities always clean.
Very tidy park,great pool and bar
Very clean and very quiet, absolutely beautiful ❤️
Required a ladder in which they had 7, to seal up a leak in my caravan roof was told no not there problem
First time in Darwin, found this park to be close enough to town via public transport stop about 150m from front door.

The cost to stay was astronomical and didn’t receive an ounce of value for money.  We had 3 bookings…. Powered site + 2 cabins.

Firstly the powered site was extremely small, we had the horrible grey water from the last caravan in our sitting area. The roads were dirt and dusty and at an average of $94 per night, with a half empty caravan park I would expect a lot more and better quality roads.

The cabins, old tired, the kitchen table in one cabin wasn’t stable enough to put drinks on confidently, there was a frog in the toilet we had to remove and the fridge leaked.  Only one bedside table and the floors in both cabins were filthy, didn’t feel like they had been swept before arrival.

The pool was dirty and got out of it because I didn’t feel comfortable. In addition, I asked if I could leave my caravan parked on the spot until 12 noon on day of departure as I was running family back to the airport and was told ‘there is no one booked on after you’ but you will need to use your late check out… as mentioned the park was half empty.  We hitched up the van and moved it outside to the street to avoid having to deal with reception again.

On each encounter with reception whether it be my family or myself we experienced aggression first and foremost and were not happy with service and text messages.

Overall on our trip so far, we have stayed at 47 locations and this one rates the worst value for money and overall experience.
Clean, well-appointed  rooms, comfortable beds, close to airport.

Location fairly average for Darwin activities. You need to like planes because you are near enough under the runway. Caravan site (131) we had was smelly and impossible to get out of our correctly booked site without the opposite site being vacant. Pool and jumping pillow kid approved.
I will be here for a week  it has been excellent so far very comfy bed 🛌 clean an cool room.  Very happy.  Tuw staff received good advice on place to eat an see sun set. 🌞
Friendly, helpful team from check in to check out. Clean, well maintained facilities.  Convenient location. Highly recommended.
We stayed in the caravan park. Amenities and pool area were clean. Customer service was excellent

Smaller powered site in back corner, longer walk to amenities,  but an advantage being away from busy exit and entry points in park. Lovely pool, clean amenities,  great location for site seeing and car service.

1 word.

GESTAPO.

After driving 7 hrs, and cracking a windscreen, I arrived in Darwin for the first time. I decided to go with G'day Discovery Parks in Darwin as the Apollo Bay Staff, ammenities and site was 5 Star. But to my disappointment Darwin was a big let down. Staff acted as if they had other problems and weren't friendly or helpful at all. I have a motor home, the site they put me on wasn't level at all. I asked if there was tree cover over the site as I have Starlink I was asking to make sure there was none. No there's no tree cover on that site. Any problems come back and we can help.  There was tree cover ! Those who know what it's like driving anything around a caravan park would realize what it's like to arrive on a site only to have to turn around and go back to reception. Total waste of my time and a fob off by staff. The staff obviously don't have a clue. To top things off the park is the most expensive caravan park I've found all the way from Melbourne, The Great Ocean Road, Adelaide,  the centre including Ayers Rock, Kings Canyon, Alice Springs, Tennant Creek (All Discovery / G'day), to Darwin and Darwin I'd list as one of the worse !!!!

Great facilities, well maintained and clean.
So nice to be in park with no dogs. It's a holiday in itself.

I can see that staff are trying hard to please and that the buildings are all being maintained to a reasonable standard, but for such a tropical location we were very disappointed at how dead all the grass is & how many sites are just gravel. While the pool areas are quite tropical I don’t think you can claim that the sites are at all. The tree cover/ shade was minimal. The toilet blocks have heaps of amenities, but like most places it was a struggle to get near a washing machine.||Overall the worst part was the constant flyovers by the military jets, which are ridiculously loud. On day one it was a bit exciting & similar to a Top Gun movie set, but after the first couple of days the novelty definitely wears off & it just becomes annoying. They are so bad that you can’t hear anything at all other than the jet engines when they pass over & then they leave you with your ears ringing after several takeoffs in close proximity. They were even flying quite late into the night most nights. I realise that Discovery parks have no control over RAAF activities, but there is no mention of it on the website anywhere when booking & the no refund after checkin policy is the only reason we didn’t cut & run after a couple of days. I would definitely recommend that you don’t stay here if there is any suggestion that RAAF are running night operations.

Great little cabin with everything I needed for a couple of days in Darwin. Close to the airport, and was a short drive to the city. Room and bathroom was clean. Bed was comfortable and had a spacious kitchen with fridge and utensils. There was adequate cookware to get by for a few dinners. Nice and quiet spot in the caravan park. Thank you for a lovely place to stay!

We were extremely disappointed with this Caravan Park. It is not well maintained, gardens are messy and neglected, laundry equipment needs repairs and it is so very noisy as close to the airport with military jets doing continuous flyovers. It is our worst experience since joining the Discovery G’Day group and certainly would not recommend to anyone - many better caravan parks to go to in Darwin.

Second time here, 1st stay in one of their cabins. Friendly staff, clean, tidy, spacious cabin. Bed was so comfy! Beautiful grounds. Despite being close to the airport it was quiet & relaxing sitting on the front verandah of the cabin. Thank you for making our stay so comfortable & relaxing. Keep up the good work!

We are staying here for 5 nights.  Unfortunately we can't get wifi in our van. We have previously stayed here and was able to get wifi.  I have a medical monitoring device that relies on wifi to send information. I contacted staff and was told it's no longer throughout the park and the best place to get it is the reception area. I told them we have stayed here before and got wifi. I was then told by staff a storm came through and it's been a problem ever sense. I told the staff member there was no wifi in Alice Springs either she said it was the same problem, the storm but Alice Springs staff said they only have routers throughout the park at specific areas. Something is not adding up. I think our days in G'Day/Discovery Parks is nearing an end unfortunately. If G'Day/Discovery Parks is no longer supplying wifi to customer tell them the truth. I pre booked both sites so had no idea the WiFi service had changed.
Great spot for the family. Only negative would be the constant planes flying over the camp. If you’re a light sleeper get some earbuds!

Stayed in a modern cabin. Nice large fridge freezer, a bit noisy but just switched it off overnight items still frozen in the morning. Fantastic shower and extremely comfy bed. I'm not the deepest of sleepers but plane noise didn't bother me, maybe loud if camping but not in the chalets. Pool was lovely.
Yes great 👍 for outdoor living the the management experience was good 👍
Awesome!

If WIFI is important don't stay here!! After travelling for 17 hours and looking forward to catching up on news and emails I arrive to find the small print on their welcome leaflet says they ' Offer LIMITED and INTERMITTENT acces for guests to check emails and search the internet'. I'm not a teenager who needs my internet acces limited.  For NZ$159 per night, this is an absolute rip-off.  I would not have booked here, to stay in what is a basic donga, and no WIFI. So now I'm having to use mobile data to do the basics.  And if you like reading by a dull yellow / brown light, well you'll be happy with the lighting. otherwise bring a torch. Unimpressed? Sure am!

El mejor camping de todo el territorio norte . Dos piscinas , mucho espacio y todas las comodidades que necesitabamos. Lastima que solo hemos pasado una noche .
Las zonas comunes estaban muy limpias.

Absolutely marvellous well kept facilities and grounds wonderful staff and knowledge gorgeous stop to stop wile taking mum on this amazing trip around Australia and this top 1 to call in spend some quality time relaxing
Enquired to this place 3 times now requesting info about accommodating a baby. No reply. Not sure they want us to stay there?

In our basic cabin we had everything we needed.  The kitchenette was well stocked with utensils and dishwares.  Very clean and the beds super comfortable.  We also enjoyed the pool on those hot Darwin afternoons.
Super friendly and helpful service. Nice clean park with two good swimming pools.

Park is located near airport but don't let that put you off. I had no problems sleeping at night. Yes you get the occasional plane etc but I never noticed any inside van in evening/night. I would suggest you check if military exercises on as the one time military Jets took off they were LOUD. This was during the day though. The location is very convenient to everything in Darwin. Park is clean and tidy and amenities were fine. Minor issue with shower head once and temp needs adjusting often .Staff helpful. Washing machines work ok. Not a lot of shade trees but that just means more awnings out.  Price was on high side and I think that is why the park wasn't full. Saying that though it had everything I needed for eight nights. I would stay again. There is also a coffee van that rocks up at 9.30 on week days

This park is directly in the flight path for the international airport in Darwin.  Our first night was interrupted continually up until at least 2.45 in the morning with overhead plane travel.. it is also THE most expensive caravan park we have stayed at at $75.00per night in Australia......truly exorbitant for nothing more than we have stayed at anywhere else.   No concrete slab, prickles  underfoot.... I get the no grass, it's Darwin in the dry, but for that price, you would expect weed management... we have moved our van to the burbs ..much better. 🙂

We have spent 10 days here and had a wonderful relaxing time. ALL the staff are kind and friendly go above and beyond for the guests.
Will come back one day
Superb accommodation. Spacious and clean cabins.  Well maintained.  Excellent service.  Keep up the good work.

It may be the closest caravan park to Darwin CBD but don't do it! Its Crazy loud being in the flight path of the military airport. One night there were helicopters and planes overhead till past midnight and started back up at 4:30am. The darwin royal show also set up right next door for 3 nights so that just turned up the noise another level. $95 a night for 2 adults and 2 kids with discount. Not worth it. 1 rustic style open air camp kitchen for that money with 1 sink. We washed our dishes one evening while 3 other people waited for us to finish. The showers had crazy low shower heads and the hot and cold taps were the wrong way round. WiFi was non existent.  For this money we have stayed at other caravan parks with state of the art facilities.

Rang and booked a few days in advance $80 a night powered site 🤔. They said nothing about the Darwin show right next door until payment was made. Not happy about horrendous noise which was supposed to stop at 10pm but went to almost 10.30. Do not come and stay here for atleast two more days it's bloody noisy and not nice. 😡😡
Absolute rip off $80 to pitch a tent. Noisy park & showers while they worked really need an upgrade.

Closest holiday park to Darwin, huge, lots f families. Good amenities, pools, good sites.
Downsides are: no proper wifi, location (industrial area without any supermarkets or restaurants) and the price. It’s crazy expensive and doesn’t even offer real wifi. But you don’t really have a choice I guess 😕

Update: Laundry is getting more dirty in the washing machine than it was before. So don’t use them. Staff didn’t care about it 🤷🏼‍♂️
They turn off the water in the park without prior warning. Bathroom are closed for cleaning around 9am 🤦🏼‍♂️
That makes it the most expansive and worst managed campsite we’ve ever been to
Would of given a 5for room/cabin but the new paint smell

Accomadation cabin was fine, bed comfy had everything we needed. Park was clean and tidy. Had 2 x pools, jumping pillow for kids.||||Close to airport so could hear planes taking off. Bit out of town not too far though.||Overall nice stay
Lovely sites...staff very helpful...
Management are aggressive and don’t like children. Literally caused so much tension and is the most expensive park we have stayed at.

We stayed in a 2 person cabin and it was new and well appointed.  Fridge/freezer instead of bar fridge was great to have. Kitchenette well kitted out with cooking utensils, crockery, cutlery and glasses. Comfortable bed, small meals table and chairs. Great base to explore Darwin and surrounding area.
Located about 10-15 from Darwin City centre. About $21-$25 by Uber. A couple of nice pools, great asmenities

We found this park really tidy and well maintained.  The pools and the amenities were really clean and all the staff were friendly.  It's out of the city a bit but that's not always a bad thing. Would definitely recommend this park if visiting Darwin.

A smaller room than last visit, didn't feel right bed directly in front of the door so after rearranging the room around a little, it was alot more cosy & comfortable. Besides that it was lovely, cool and clean on arrival. Advertised with balcony, unfortunately it wasn't a balcony. Not enough tea n coffee for 2 nights either which bit of a bummer. Overall it once again was a nice place to stay, friendly staff on arrival, peaceful, comfortable and we would recommend

2nd time back here staying at the opposite end of the park in a drive through site. The pool was most welcome after the long drive and the hot humid afternoon of setting up .Still a great park with good clean amenities and plenty to offer with its location.
Great caravan park, friendly, helpful and accommodating staff. Highly recommended. Great site 120, lots of grass for the dog. Great location to score Darwin and surrounds. Very safe!
Very clean well run park, although it's close to the airport the plane's don't fly at night and if they I didn't hear any.

Very big park, 2 pools, clean amenities nice and green.
Nice friendly staff.
Dogs allowed in own van.

Very noisy. The cabin I stayed in was lovely and the site is really nice but the noise from the airport, mostly the helicopters that operate from just across the road is incredible. They literally shake the windows. Keep that in mind when looking at the premium price.

About 10km out of Darwin it didn't take long to get to the CBD. The local bus stop is just outside which we used a couple of times although they don't run very late at night for an evening out.|We learnt after the first night that we had to buy ear plugs to get a descent nights sleep as the noise from the airport and the Stuart Highway day and night is not good.|The van park sites and facilities very good.
